text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Elderglen, there stood an ancient oak tree rumored to be centuries old. The villagers would gather under its broad branches on quiet evenings to hear tales of ancient magic.

There was Olive, an elderly woman known for her love of collecting wildlife souvenirs. She claimed that every animal she found had secrets only it could tell. On a crisp autumn morning, Olive ventured beneath the oak tree, drawn by its peculiar hum.

Suddenly, a soft voice drifted through the leaves. It was soothing, intelligent. Olive looked up to see a wise owl perched gracefully on the tree branch. The owl fluttered down closer to her, eyes twinkling with mystery. In a gentle, gravelly voice, the owl began to tell her about the guardians of the woods.

According to the owl, each year a rare bird would visit Elderglen during the full moon. This bird, known only as the Whispering Nightjar, whispered secrets only to those pure
